reference: Hi. I am thirty-two years old, with a Bachelor of Medicine, Bachelor of Surgery. I had two lower segment cesarean sections, the last one being a year and a half ago. I have severe pain in my back and abdomen. I was diagnosed with gallstones in February two thousand and fourteen when there was a single stone measuring nine point two millimeters. I have had repeated episodes of pain, approximately ten times since February, with the last episode on August thirteen. The pain always occurs around three:zero AM. I had a repeated scan on August fourteen, which showed two stones measuring eight millimeters and nine millimeters. I started taking Udiliv yesterday. Will it be effective? I want to delay surgery. Is surgery the only treatment? If yes, can it be delayed? Please help.
text: Hi, I am thirty two years old with a bachelor of medicine, bachelor of surgery, and have two lower cervical sphenoid fractures, plus from being the heel of a golf ball. I have severe pain in my back in the afternoon. I was diagnosed with two stones in February two thousand and fourteen, when there was a single stone measuring nine point two millimeters. I have had repeated episodes of pain, approximately ten times in February, with the last episode on August eight. The pain always occurs around three zero AM, and had a repeated scan on August fourteen, which showed two stones measuring eight millimeters and nine millimeters. I started taking a dose yesterday. Will it be effective? One to two days. The surgery, the only treatment, S, can be delayed. Two hours.
